description: Create SnowFilter and BloodFilter GLSL shader filters in :game, and a prototype test scene (PhysicsTestScene) that demonstrates all 5 systems working together. Add screenshot harness captures for CI verification. Integrate with ShaderEffects registry. Torch light uses existing LightingFilter + LightSource. Wind drives RainFilter's windAngle and SnowFilter's drift direction. The scene is a standalone demo level (not wired into WorldScene gameplay yet).
steps: Create game/src/desktopMain/kotlin/game/shader/SnowFilter.kt: ShaderFilter that renders a white overlay with alpha proportional to snow depth. Uses a uniform for a small tile-grid sample (8x8 floats packed into vec4 uniforms) plus wind direction to animate falling snow particles. UniformBlock fixedLocation=11. Fragment shader: white overlay (alpha = snowDepth per pixel region) + procedural falling snow particles drifting with wind.

Create game/src/desktopMain/kotlin/game/shader/BloodFilter.kt: ShaderFilter that renders dark red splatters. Uses a uniform for a small tile-grid sample (8x8 packed). UniformBlock fixedLocation=12. Fragment shader: red-black overlay (alpha = bloodLevel per pixel region) with splatter-edge noise pattern.

Update game/src/desktopMain/kotlin/game/shader/ShaderEffects.kt: Add snowFilter and bloodFilter instances. Update startTimeUpdater to drive time on both new filters. Add attachSnow(target) and attachBlood(target) methods.

Create game/src/desktopMain/kotlin/game/PhysicsTestScene.kt: A self-contained test scene that: (a) Creates a WeatherState from :core with a 13x8 grid (matching 640x360 at 48px tiles). (b) Configures wind blowing from the left (angle ~0.3 rad). (c) Runs snow accumulation for simulated 10 seconds (fills snowGrid to ~0.5). (d) Stamps footprints in a path (3-4 tiles). (e) Splatters blood at 2 locations. (f) Places 2 torch LightSources (warm flickering candles). (g) Applies SnowFilter on the map layer, BloodFilter as additive, LightingFilter with torches. (h) Renders a TMX map (reuses exterior map) as the base. The scene is deterministic (all values fixed, time frozen at specific moment).

Add capturePhysicsSnow(), capturePhysicsBlood(), capturePhysicsFootprints(), capturePhysicsTorchlight(), capturePhysicsComplete() functions to ScreenshotHarness.kt. Each isolates one system for clarity, the 'complete' one shows all 5 together. All use frozen time values and deterministic grid states. CRITICAL: use localCurrentDirVfs (not localVfs) for output path (B007 pitfall).
Call the 5 new capture functions from the existing main() function in ScreenshotHarness.kt.
Ensure RainFilter windAngle is driven by WindState.angle in PhysicsTestScene (demonstrating wind system drives both rain direction and snow drift).
acceptance_criteria: ./gradlew :game:compileKotlinDesktop succeeds with new filters and scene
SnowFilter renders white overlay proportional to grid depth values
BloodFilter renders red overlay proportional to grid blood values
PhysicsTestScene integrates all 5 systems (snow + footprints + wind + blood + torch light)
5 new screenshot captures added to harness (physics_snow, physics_blood, physics_footprints, physics_torchlight, physics_complete)
Screenshot harness uses localCurrentDirVfs for output (not localVfs)
verification: ./gradlew :game:screenshot (runs, produces PNGs including the 5 new physics_*.png files > 1KB each)
